description: Build a gated Data Catalog Portal on the Claru landing site (Next.js 16, App Router, Tailwind CSS 4, Supabase) with three surfaces: public landing page with access request form, admin dashboard for lead and dataset management, and authenticated lead portal for browsing a curated catalog of AI training data with video sample viewers. Uses Supabase Auth (magic link for leads), RLS, Storage, and Resend for transactional email.

userStories.title: Set up Supabase client and environment config
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Supabase client utilities and environment configuration so that all subsequent stories can interact with Supabase services.
acceptanceCriteria: src/lib/supabase/server.ts created with createSupabaseServerClient() using @supabase/ssr and cookies() from next/headers -- matching design doc Section 5.1
src/lib/supabase/admin.ts created with createSupabaseAdminClient() using @supabase/supabase-js with SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY -- matching design doc Section 5.1
Dependencies installed: @supabase/supabase-js, @supabase/ssr
.env.local.example updated with required Supabase env vars: NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL, NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY, SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY
next.config.ts updated: add Supabase Storage hostname to images.remotePatterns for <Image> support
Both client functions export correctly and can be imported without build errors
npm run build succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create database tables and RLS policies via Supabase migration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Postgres schema with all tables, indexes, triggers, and RLS policies so that the data layer is ready for application code.
acceptanceCriteria: SQL migration file created at supabase/migrations/001_data_catalog_schema.sql

dataset_categories table created with columns: id (UUID PK), name (text UNIQUE NOT NULL), slug (text UNIQUE NOT NULL), description (text), sort_order (integer default 0), created_at (timestamptz) -- per design doc Section 4.1

datasets table created with columns: id, name, slug (UNIQUE), description, category_id (FK), modality (CHECK in video/image/multimodal/robotics), thumbnail_path, total_size, sample_count (default 0), metadata (jsonb), is_public (boolean default false), is_archived (boolean default false), created_at, updated_at -- per design doc Section 4.1

dataset_samples table created with columns: id, dataset_id (FK ON DELETE CASCADE), name, storage_path, file_type, file_size (bigint), metadata (jsonb), sort_order, created_at -- per design doc Section 4.1

leads table created with columns: id, email (UNIQUE), name, company, role, use_case, modalities_of_interest (text[]), category_ids_of_interest (uuid[]), volume_notes, status (CHECK in pending/approved/rejected default 'pending'), admin_notes, rejection_reason, supabase_user_id (UNIQUE nullable), created_at, updated_at -- per design doc Section 4.1

lead_dataset_access junction table created with columns: id, lead_id (FK ON DELETE CASCADE), dataset_id (FK ON DELETE CASCADE), granted_at, granted_by (text), UNIQUE on (lead_id, dataset_id) -- per design doc Section 4.1

All RLS policies created per design doc Section 4.2: public read on categories, public read on public non-archived datasets, lead reads on granted datasets/samples/own record/own access grants, service_role full access
All indexes created per design doc Section 4.3
updated_at trigger function created for leads and datasets tables
Migration is idempotent (uses IF NOT EXISTS or similar guards)
npm run build succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Set up Supabase Storage bucket with access policies
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Supabase Storage buckets configured with correct access policies so that dataset thumbnails and video samples can be stored and served securely.
acceptanceCriteria: SQL migration or config creates a dataset-samples private storage bucket
Storage policy: only service_role can manage files (all reads via signed URLs) -- per design doc Section 8.4
File path convention documented: {dataset_id}/thumbnail.jpg for thumbnails, {dataset_id}/samples/{sample_id}/{filename} for samples -- per design doc Section 8.1
Signed URL generation utility created in src/lib/supabase/storage.ts with generateSignedUrl(storagePath, expiresIn?) defaulting to 3600s and generateSignedUploadUrl(storagePath) for admin uploads
No max file size limit enforced on uploads
npm run build succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create TypeScript types for all database entities
userStories.description: As a developer, I need TypeScript interfaces for all database entities and API payloads so that the entire codebase has type safety.
acceptanceCriteria: src/types/catalog.ts created with interfaces: DatasetCategory, Dataset, DatasetSample, Lead, LeadDatasetAccess, LeadStatus type, DatasetModality type -- all matching design doc Section 4.1
src/lib/supabase/types.ts created with Supabase database types
API request/response types created: AccessRequestPayload, LeadApprovePayload, LeadRejectPayload, DatasetCreatePayload, SampleUploadUrlPayload, SampleUploadUrlResponse -- per design doc Section 5
All types align with design doc schema (column names, types, nullability match)
npm run build succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Data Catalog landing page /data-catalog
userStories.description: As a potential customer, I want to see an overview of Claru's available dataset categories with volume and quality stats so I can understand what data is available before requesting access. This is a category-level overview + request access page only -- no individual dataset browsing on the public page.
acceptanceCriteria: Page at src/app/data-catalog/page.tsx -- Server Component fetching dataset categories with aggregate stats using createSupabaseAdminClient
Uses shared Header (with opaque prop) and Footer components
Hero section with headline, subheadline, and primary Request Access CTA button
Grid of category overview cards (not individual dataset cards) showing: category name, dataset count, total hours/clips, representative thumbnail
Clicking a category card smooth-scrolls to a section with high-level category stats and data types
Clicking Request Access CTA navigates to /data-catalog/request
Server-rendered with meta tags: title, description, OG image for SEO
Fully responsive: single-column card grid on mobile (<768px)
Design matches existing dark terminal aesthetic (bg --bg-primary, accent --accent-primary, fonts Geist Sans / JetBrains Mono)
DatasetCard component created at src/app/components/ui/DatasetCard.tsx with variant: public | portal
npm run build succeeds
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Access request form with multi-step qualification
userStories.description: As a potential customer, I want to submit a request for catalog access with my details and intended use case so that Claru can evaluate and grant me access.
acceptanceCriteria: Page at src/app/data-catalog/request/page.tsx (Server Component wrapper) + RequestFormClient.tsx (Client Component)
Server Component pre-fetches categories from Supabase and passes as props

Multi-step terminal-style form matching existing ContactForm pattern: Step 1 contact info (name, email, company, role), Step 2 use case (textarea min 20 chars + modality checkboxes), Step 3 dataset interests (multi-select categories), Step 4 volume/timeline, Step 5 confirm/submit
Validation via React Hook Form + Zod: email validation, required fields, min length on use case
Inline validation errors using terminal-style form patterns
Success confirmation message after submission with expected response timeline
Uses shared Header (with opaque prop) and Footer components
Responsive layout on all viewports
npm run build succeeds
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Form submission Server Action + Supabase insert
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the form submission to securely write to Supabase via a Next.js Server Action, keeping credentials server-side.
acceptanceCriteria: Server Action or API route at src/app/api/leads/route.ts for POST /api/leads
Uses createSupabaseAdminClient (service_role) to insert into leads table
On success: inserts row with status pending and created_at timestamp
On duplicate email: updates existing lead record; resets status to pending if previously rejected
On Supabase insert failure: returns user-friendly error message and logs server-side
Zod validation on server side (same schema as client)
Also creates GET /api/catalog/categories/route.ts for public categories list
Returns 201 { success: true, message: 'Request submitted' } on success
npm run build succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Email notification to admin on new request (Resend)
userStories.description: As a Claru admin, I want to be notified by email when a new access request comes in so I don't have to manually check the admin dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: resend dependency installed
src/lib/email.ts created with sendEmail() utility -- sender: Claru AI <team@claru.ai>

Admin notification email sent on new lead submission: subject '[Claru] New data catalog request from {company}', body contains lead name, company, email, use case summary, modalities, link to /admin/leads/{id}
Email sent to team@claru.ai (admin notification address)
.env.local.example updated with RESEND_API_KEY and ADMIN_NOTIFICATION_EMAIL=team@claru.ai
If email delivery fails: error logged but does not block lead insert
Email templates use dark terminal aesthetic: dark background (#0a0908), sage green accents (#92B090), JetBrains Mono font
All four email template functions created (admin notification, lead approved, lead rejected, new datasets granted) using consistent dark terminal style
Email send called from the POST /api/leads route handler created in US-007
npm run build succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Admin leads list page /admin/leads
userStories.description: As a Claru admin, I want to view all access requests in a filterable table so I can efficiently triage incoming leads.
acceptanceCriteria: Page at src/app/admin/leads/page.tsx -- Server Component + Client Component interactive table
Protected by existing JWT cookie middleware; redirects unauthenticated users to admin login
Table columns: name, email, company, status (LeadStatusBadge), submitted date, action link
LeadStatusBadge component at src/app/components/ui/LeadStatusBadge.tsx with color mapping: pending=warning, approved=accent-primary, rejected=error
Default sort: created_at descending (newest first)
Status filter tabs: All / Pending / Approved / Rejected
Search input filters by name, email, or company (case-insensitive, debounced 300ms)
Pagination: 25 rows per page with previous/next controls
Click row navigates to /admin/leads/[id]
Matches existing AdminJobsTable pattern
Data fetched via createSupabaseAdminClient

userStories.title: Admin lead detail page /admin/leads/[id] with approve/reject
userStories.description: As a Claru admin, I want to view a lead's full details and approve or reject their access request so I can control who gets portal access.
acceptanceCriteria: Page at src/app/admin/leads/[id]/page.tsx -- Server Component + Client sub-components
Displays lead's full info: name, email, company, role, categories of interest, use case, submission date, current status with LeadStatusBadge
Approve button updates lead status to approved via API call
Reject button updates lead status to rejected with optional rejection reason textarea
Admin notes textarea for internal notes
404 page displayed for non-existent lead IDs
API routes created: POST /api/admin/leads/[id]/approve, POST /api/admin/leads/[id]/reject, GET /api/admin/leads/[id]
Approve route: updates status, creates Supabase Auth user, stores supabase_user_id, generates magic link, sends approval email
Reject route: updates status, sends rejection email via Resend
Approval email contains welcome message, magic link button, dataset count
Rejection email: professional message, optional rejection reason, link to contact form

userStories.title: Lead approval email with magic link (Resend + Supabase Auth)
userStories.description: As an approved lead, I want to receive an email with a magic link that signs me into the portal so that I don't need to manage a password.
acceptanceCriteria: Approve API route creates Supabase Auth user: supabaseAdmin.auth.admin.createUser({ email, email_confirm: true })
supabase_user_id from created auth user stored on lead record
Magic link generated via supabaseAdmin.auth.admin.generateLink({ type: 'magiclink', email, options: { redirectTo: '${SITE_URL}/portal' } })
Magic link URL included in approval email sent via Resend with Access Your Portal button
Approval email includes welcome message, dataset count, note that link expires
Rejection email sent on reject action: professional message, optional rejection reason, contact form link
New datasets granted email: list of newly granted datasets + magic link
If magic link user already exists (re-approval), generate new magic link instead of creating duplicate user

Edit page at src/app/admin/catalog/[id]/page.tsx (Server Component + Client sub-components)

Form fields: name (required), slug (auto-generated, editable), description (markdown textarea), category (select), modality (select: video/image/multimodal/robotics), metadata (JSON editor textarea), isPublic toggle
Edit form pre-populated with existing dataset data including current thumbnail and video previews
Delete button triggers confirmation dialog, then soft-deletes (sets is_archived = true)
API routes: POST /api/admin/catalog (create), PUT /api/admin/catalog/[id] (update), DELETE /api/admin/catalog/[id] (archive)

userStories.title: Dataset sample upload to Supabase Storage
userStories.description: As a Claru admin, I want to upload thumbnails and video samples to datasets via drag-and-drop so that leads can preview data quality.
acceptanceCriteria: DatasetUploader component created at src/app/components/ui/DatasetUploader.tsx
Supports drag-and-drop and file picker for uploads
Accepted file types: .mp4, .mov, .webm, .jpg, .png, .json
Upload flow: client requests signed upload URL, uploads directly to Supabase Storage, then calls API to create DB record

API routes: POST /api/admin/catalog/[id]/samples/upload-url (signed URL), POST /api/admin/catalog/[id]/samples (DB record), DELETE /api/admin/catalog/[id]/samples/[sampleId] (delete from Storage + DB)
Terminal-style progress bar during upload
Thumbnail upload stores URL in datasets.thumbnail_path
Video sample upload stores URL in dataset_samples table
Samples grid on edit page shows uploaded samples with thumbnails, file info, delete button

userStories.title: Seed initial datasets from Notion catalog data
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a seed script that populates the database with initial dataset categories and datasets from the existing Notion catalog.
acceptanceCriteria: Script at scripts/seed-datasets.ts (runnable with npx tsx scripts/seed-datasets.ts)
Creates dataset categories: Egocentric Crowd, Egocentric Workplaces, Licensed Cinematic, Car Footage

Creates dataset records for each Notion entry mapping columns to design doc schema: name, category_id, modality, metadata (jsonb with Type/Subcategory/S3 URL/Drive links), is_public=true for representative subset
Uses createSupabaseAdminClient for all inserts (service_role, bypasses RLS)
Script is idempotent: running again does not create duplicates (upsert or existence check)
Script logs progress to console
npm run build succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Lead authentication middleware (Supabase magic link session)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need middleware that protects all /portal/* routes by checking for a valid Supabase session and validates that the lead's status is still approved.
acceptanceCriteria: src/middleware.ts updated to handle /portal/* routes alongside existing /admin/* routes
Portal route middleware creates Supabase server client and calls supabase.auth.getSession()
If no session: redirect to /data-catalog?auth=required
If session exists but lead status is not approved: display message that request is under review, deny portal access
Portal login page at src/app/portal/login/page.tsx with email input and Send Magic Link button
Login form calls supabase.auth.signInWithOtp({ email })
No information leakage: always show generic 'check your email' message regardless of email existence
Magic link callback sets session and redirects to /portal
Session expiry redirects to /data-catalog?auth=expired
If lead status changed from approved to rejected, portal access invalidated on next request
Middleware matcher updated to include portal routes
npm run build succeeds
Typecheck passes

Error boundary at src/app/portal/error.tsx with terminal-style error screen
